**Conflict of Interest**

The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

**Data Availability Statement**

Data will be made available on request.

**Author Information and Contribution**

Momanyi Amos Okirigiti: Conceptualization, Methodology, Investigation, Data curation, Formal analysis, Visualization, Writing – original draft. Cheol Min Kim: Methodology, Formal analysis, Validation. Hyejeong Choi: Methodology, Formal analysis, Validation. Nagamalleswara Rao Alluri: Data curation, Formal analysis, review & editing. Kwi-Il Park: Conceptualization, Writing – review & editing, Supervision, Resources, Funding acquisition, Project administration.

IMAGE 1:
URL: https://pub.mdpi-res.com/energies/energies-15-07307/article_deploy/html/images/energies-15-07307-g017.png?1665561976
DESCRIPTION: The content showcases advancements in thermoelectric materials technology, featuring a thermoelectric module alongside graphs illustrating conversion efficiency, maximum output power, maximum temperature difference, and cooling power as functions of current and temperature difference.

IMAGE 2:
URL: https://pub.mdpi-res.com/energies/energies-17-05002/article_deploy/html/images/energies-17-05002-g001.png?1728387483
DESCRIPTION: A diagram compares two thermoelectric characterization techniques: the Seeback effect in power generation mode on the left, showing heat-induced charge flow from hot to cold sides, and the Peltier effect in refrigeration mode on the right, illustrating current-driven heat transfer with heat dissipation.

IMAGE 3:
URL: https://spj.science.org/cms/10.34133/energymatadv.0124/asset/35e0aade-a829-4a6f-ba48-148b62b8d10e/assets/graphic/energymatadv.0124.fig.001.jpg
DESCRIPTION: None

URL: https://d3i71xaburhd42.cloudfront.net/e22785637efead184037250155c503b06759996b/4-Figure3-1.png
DESCRIPTION: The illustration depicts multiple schematic diagrams of advanced characterization techniques for thermoelectric materials, including setups with pressure controllers, inkjet printing systems, roll-to-roll processes, and laser ablation methods, highlighting the integration of electronic, thermal, and mechanical components.

IMAGE 5:
URL: https://pubs.rsc.org/image/article/2024/RA/d4ra03625e/d4ra03625e-f5_hi-res.gif
DESCRIPTION: The image presents schematic diagrams and experimental setups illustrating advancements in thermoelectric material characterization techniques, including a high-pressure testing device and growth methods for oxide powders, alongside graphs showing thermoelectric properties like power factor and performance metrics across temperature ranges.

enriched_points.expanded_content: A comprehensive literature review identifies recent advancements and methodologies in thermoelectric materials, providing insights into performance metrics and material innovations, as highlighted in various research articles.

Employing advanced characterization techniques, such as Seebeck and Peltier effects, researchers evaluate thermoelectric properties, enhancing understanding of electrical conductivity and thermal management in materials.

Experimental setups utilize innovative methods, including high-pressure testing and laser ablation, to assess thermoelectric performance, facilitating the development of new materials with improved efficiency.
